publicV1.reason: Task reads gain the additive nullable provisioning.progress object (percent, receivedObjects/totalObjects, receivedBytes, throughput) on TaskProvisioningSummary; emission stays behind the deployment capability gate for mixed-version rollout and payloads without the field still parse.

mcp.reason: MCP task tools project the same canonical Task response, so the additive provisioning.progress object surfaces identically through create/list/get/stop task reads with no tool-specific divergence.

openapi.reason: The OpenAPI document derives the additive TaskProvisioningProgress schema and its nullable projection on task responses from the shared public contract manifest.

apiPlayground.reason: The API Playground derives request and response rendering for the additive progress object from the public contract manifest.

internalOnly.reason: Adds the sandbox-core detached-job primitive (setsid launch, wrapper wait, pid/progress/exit markers, atomic workspace publish, kill contract), detached workspace transfer with polling execs, admission claim parking with new-lease re-claim fencing, dual-gate liveness for the transfer stage, boot marker-probe triage split from tmux re-adoption, and parked-aware stop.

protocolDifferences.detail: Idempotency-Key remains an HTTP-only retry contract and is not an MCP argument.
MCP structured output matches the canonical Task while compatibility text retains its historical task handle.
The MCP description explains asynchronous polling without claiming support for the HTTP-only idempotency header.
REST keeps its dedicated task-create throttle while MCP remains subject to the MCP transport limiter.
